package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for api_error_type_outside_transport_package.
// Rule intent: API-facing error payload structs should live in transport-boundary packages,
// not in handler/service/repository packages.
// Anti-pattern: APIError struct defined directly in the handler package (non-transport boundary).

type APIError struct {
	Code    int    `json:"code"`
	Message string `json:"message"`
	Details string `json:"details,omitempty"`
}

type UserHandler struct{}

func (h *UserHandler) GetUser(c *gin.Context) {
	id := c.Param("id")
	if id == "" {
		errResp := APIError{
			Code:    http.StatusBadRequest,
			Message: "missing user id",
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, errResp)
		return
	}

	errResp := APIError{
		Code:    http.StatusNotFound,
		Message: "user not found",
		Details: "no user exists with id " + id,
	}
	c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, errResp)
}

func (h *UserHandler) CreateUser(c *gin.Context) {
	errResp := APIError{
		Code:    http.StatusConflict,
		Message: "user already exists",
	}
	c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, errResp)
}
